data.content_ref: inline:contradicts: The volcanic cooling mechanism (639a936e) implies a short-term, acute climatic shock, whereas the prolonged drought mechanism (58e798a0) requires a sustained multi-decadal climatic anomaly; they are refuted if the climatic signatures of a volcanic winter (e.g., sulfate spikes, rapid onset, 2-5 year duration) can be shown to be indistinguishable from the proxy signatures of a centennial-scale megadrought in the same region.
